title: 'Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny' and the Indy Movie Rankings
format: Sean, Amanda, and Mallory Rubin review Harrison Ford's fifth and final outing, then rank all five Indiana Jones movies.

segments.heading: 'Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny'

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny
films.note: James Mangold takes over from Spielberg for an 80-year-old Ford's send-off. Mallory and Amanda love it top to bottom; Sean is with them through the de-aged 1944 prologue and the 1969 moon-landing setting, then cools on the Syracuse time-travel third act and its CGI. Phoebe Waller-Bridge as Helena and the Marion reunion win over all three; the retconned death of Shia LaBeouf's Mutt doesn't.

films.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark
films.note: No. 1 - '1A,' the sacred text. Sean insists it should have been an autogreen in the Hall of Fame; even Amanda concedes it.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
films.note: No. 2 - '1B.' Amanda's personal favorite; the Grail-vs-Ark 'is it in the Bible' argument runs the whole segment.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
films.note: No. 3 - gets it on historic weight over Dial, despite Willie and the culturally insensitive material. Practical Spielberg set pieces carry it.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Dial of Destiny
films.note: No. 4 - 'higher in my heart while being at four.' More fulfilling than Temple, but bloated and burdened by the whole franchise's weight.

films.title: Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull
films.note: No. 5 - not as bad on rewatch as remembered for an hour, then the last 45 minutes and the alien reveal sink it. A huge misfire at the end.
